expectations.text: DashboardLayout이 folder 기반 index.tsx 패턴으로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: Output is a single flat file 'DashboardLayout.tsx'. Transcript confirms 'refactored into clear sub-components within the same file'. No folder-based index.tsx pattern was created.

Transcript Step 5 Output states 'Modified: components/DashboardLayout.tsx' — the flat file was modified in-place rather than removed and replaced with a folder structure. The output file is still DashboardLayout.tsx at the flat level.
A dedicated 'Sidebar' function component exists (line 80) rendering an <aside> element with data-testid='dashboard-sidebar', containing logo, navigation, and footer sections.
A dedicated 'DashboardHeader' function component exists (line 149) rendering a <header> element with data-testid='dashboard-header', containing title and right slot.
A <main> element exists at line 258 with data-testid='dashboard-main' that renders {children}.
DashboardLayout accepts 'children: ReactNode' via DashboardLayoutProps (line 7) and renders it inside the <main> element (line 262). The component is a pure shell/orchestrator.

Responsive classes are present throughout: sidebar uses 'hidden md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64', header uses 'md:hidden' and 'md:block' for mobile/desktop logos, MobileNav uses 'md:hidden', main content uses 'md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8'.

expectations.text: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: No folder restructuring occurred, so there is no new path to update imports to. The transcript mentions the file was modified in-place at 'components/DashboardLayout.tsx'. No import update was needed or performed because the path didn't change, but the assertion implies a folder move should have happened.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched the entire output file — none of useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation appear anywhere. The component is purely presentational.
No folder was created. The output is a single flat file 'DashboardLayout.tsx', so there is no folder name to evaluate for camelCase convention.
The file remains at 'components/DashboardLayout.tsx' per the transcript. It was not moved to 'components/dashboard/' or any subdirectory.

claims.evidence: Cannot directly verify the original file, but the output file is 269 lines — plausible that the original monolithic version was shorter before sub-component extraction added structure.

claims.claim: Refactored into clear sub-components within the same file
claims.evidence: Output contains Sidebar (line 80), DashboardHeader (line 149), and MobileNav (line 215) as separate function components within the same file, with section separator comments.

claims.claim: All data-testid attributes preserved for test compatibility
Zero type errors and zero lint errors in the file
claims.evidence: Cannot independently verify — would need to run tsc and lint on the output. Transcript claims this but we cannot confirm from outputs alone.

claims.claim: Discovered an existing split version under components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 2 documents this discovery, but the executor chose to refactor the flat file in-place rather than completing or using the existing folder-based structure.

suggestions.assertion: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
suggestions.reason: This assertion is ambiguous when no folder restructuring occurs. It could be interpreted as 'not applicable' (vacuously true) or as a failure proxy for the restructuring itself. Consider making it explicitly depend on folder-structure passing, or test for a concrete import path like 'import DashboardLayout from components/dashboard/dashboardLayout'.

The executor discovered an existing folder-based split at components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ but chose to refactor the flat file instead. No assertion checks whether the executor should consolidate with or leverage existing code. An assertion like 'the existing components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ folder is used or cleaned up' would catch this divergence.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions cover structural placement well (5 of 11 relate to folder/path conventions). The main gap is that when the executor skips folder restructuring entirely, many assertions fail together without distinguishing root cause from downstream effects.
